reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given markdown content into Spanish, preserving frontmatter, code blocks, MDX components, etc. Must translate frontmatter values: language? There's no language field currently. Should we add language field? The rules: translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit. There's no language field; we can add language: "Spanish"? The original frontmatter doesn't have language. Should we add? The rule: If a language field exists, set it exactly to "Spanish". It doesn't exist, so we don't need to add. We need to translate title and subTitle. Keep other keys unchanged.

So frontmatter:

title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" -> translate to Spanish, preserving quotes. Something like "Es hora de las cadenas de conexión llm://". subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" -> "Simplifica la configuración de modelos y proveedores con URLs `llm://`". Keep date, modified, tags, etc unchanged.

Now body: translate prose, keep code blocks unchanged. Also need to translate blockquote text. The blockquote with class "inset" contains English text; translate it. Also other blockquotes.

Also need to translate image alt text? There's an image with alt text: "the parts of a LLM connection string". That's reader-facing alt text, should be translated. Also later alt: "a messy env var drawer". Translate.

Also there is a blockquote with class "ai-response inset" containing English; translate.

Also there is a comment with {/* ... */} which is a JSX comment; inside is English footnote. Should we translate? It's inside comment, not displayed. Probably not needed, but it's reader-facing? It's a comment, not displayed. Might keep as is. But could translate? The rule: preserve all code, MDX components, only translate string prop values that are reader-facing. The comment is not a prop; it's just comment. Probably keep as is.

Also there is a blockquote with class "inset" earlier: "Update: This article led to an [Internet-Draft ...]" Translate that.

Also need to prefix relative URLs with ../. The image src is "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". Should become ".././inline-url-diagram-dark.svg"? Actually relative path should be prefixed with ../. So "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg" becomes ".././inline-url-diagram-dark.svg"? Usually you would do "../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". The rule: All relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`. So we need to add ../ before the path. If path already starts with ./, we add ../ before it: ".././inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". But maybe better to replace "./" with "../". We'll follow rule: prefix with ../, so "./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg" -> ".././inline-url-diagram-dark.svg". Similarly for "./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp". Also for cover images in frontmatter: cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp etc. Those are frontmatter values not reader-facing? They are filenames, not reader-facing. Should keep unchanged but maybe need to prefix with ../? The rule: preserve filenames but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders. Since these are frontmatter values, they are not reader-facing, but they are asset paths. The rule says preserve filenames but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders. So we should change cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp to .././hero-wide.webp? Probably yes. But they are not reader-facing, but still asset paths. The rule: "Preserve social_image, cover, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon filenames, but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders." So we need to add ../ prefix. So change each to ".././hero-wide.webp" etc.

Now translate the blockquote "Update: This article led to an [Internet-Draft ...]" The link text "Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme" should be translated? The link text is "Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme". Translate to Spanish: "Borrador de Internet para el esquema URI `llm://`". Keep URL unchanged.

Now translate the rest.

Let's produce final output.
